last_user_message:
## Eval Round 1 came back REJECT — fix the BLOCKERs. Read the full report yourself first. Read `/app/clone-web/team-log/eval_round_1.md` and `/app/clone-web/team-log/eval_raw.json`. The eval report has the per-AC table, numeric evidence (orig vs clone), and a prioritized fix list with file/line citations. Apply the fixes below. The dev server is STILL RUNNING on http://127.0.0.1:5174/ — reuse it. Don't restart unless it's down. ### ADJUDICATION — the evaluator's extractor was buggy on some points; here's what's real vs what to re-verify **The user's request defines the "tabs":** "the multi-tab interface for selecting Passenger Cars, Trucks, Motorcycles, etc., by make, model, and engine." That means the **vehicle-TYPE switcher** (Vehículo de turismo / Camión / Motocicleta) is the tab interface the user cares about — it is a SEPARATE bar from the dark category sub-nav. You (builder) MERGED Camión + Motocicleta into the dark category sub-nav. That's the core structural error. **BLOCKER #1 (AC-2 sub-nav) — RE-VERIFY against live DOM, then fix.** Write a quick Playwright re-extraction that, after Cloudflare clears + cookie overlay removed, captures the EXACT two bars separately: (a) the **vehicle-type switcher** bar — find the element showing "Vehículo de turismo" with a dropdown/expand that offers Camión + Motocicleta. Capture its exact items, order, and how switching works (does it link to camiones.autodoc.es / moto.autodoc.es, or expand inline?). (b) the **dark category sub-nav** (`
    ` or equivalent) — capture its exact items + order + count (the evaluator says 9, you said 10). Save both to `tokens.json` under `structure.vehicleTypeTabs` and `structure.categorySubnav`. Then rebuild the clone so these are TWO separate, correctly-populated bars matching the live site exactly. The vehicle-type bar must present Vehículo de turismo / Camión / Motocicleta as switchable tabs (the user's explicit "Passenger Cars, Trucks, Motorcycles" requirement). The category sub-nav must have exactly the items the live site has, in order — no more, no less. **BLOCKER #2/#5/#6 (AC-5/AC-6/AC-10 — CTA always blue) — REAL, fix directly.** In `SelectorCard.jsx`, the bottom `Buscar` CTA uses `disabled={!canSearch}` + a conditional `backgroundColor` that turns it gray `#9C9C9C` at first paint. The original keeps it bright blue `#0068D7` at ALL times (only `#0074F1` on hover). Fix: the CTA must ALWAYS render `backgroundColor: '#0068D7'`, hover `#0074F1`. It may stay semantically `disabled` (not submit without selection) but visually it is ALWAYS blue — never gray. Also: set explicit `height: '48px'` and `padding: '0 24px'` and `borderRadius: '1.86px'` on the bottom Buscar so it matches the original's 48px height + centered text + exact radius. Verify with a Playwright computed-style read on the clone. **BLOCKER #3 (AC-8 semantic `
    `) — REAL, trivial fix.** `Header.jsx` wraps in `
    ` — change the outer wrapper to a semantic `
    ` element. Keep the logo `` inside it. This fixes both screen-reader landmarks and the extractor. **BLOCKER #4 (AC-1 banner width +73px) — REAL, fix.** `Hero.jsx` uses `grid-cols-[minmax(0,570px)_1fr]` which lets the banner fill all remaining space. The original banner is fixed ~735px wide. Re-extract the live banner's exact width + the selector card's exact width + the gap between them, then set explicit column widths (e.g. `grid-cols-[px_px]` with the real gap) so the banner matches the original's width within ±4px. Also verify at 1920px the whole hero centers (max-width container) — the evaluator noted the banner didn't recenter. ### MAJOR / MINOR (apply all) - **AC-9 dot count 10 for 9 slides** — fix `BannerCarousel.jsx` so the dot list renders exactly `SLIDES.length` dots (likely a key/reconciliation bug or an extra sibling). Verify in DOM: `document.querySelectorAll('.slick-dots li').length === SLIDES.length`. - **AC-9 dot size** — change `.slick-dots li` in `index.css` from 8×8 to 12×12 (per tokens). - **AC-9 `data-timeout="3"`** — add it as a literal attribute on the carousel root div. - **AC-2 sub-nav font** — `text-[12px]` → `text-[13px]` in `CategoryNav.jsx`. - **AC-9 autoplay runtime** — make sure the autoplay `useEffect` actually fires on mount (the evaluator couldn't confirm at runtime). After your fix, run a Playwright check: capture active slide index, wait 4000ms, capture again, assert it changed. ### Process 1. Re-extract the two nav bars from live DOM (BLOCKER #1 adjudication). 2. Apply all fixes (surgical edits — don't rewrite components wholesale). 3. `npm run build` must still exit 0. 4. Run a Playwright self-check on the clone (clean navigation, no prior state): verify (a) CTA bg `#0068D7` at first paint, (b) CTA height 48px, (c) semantic `
    ` present, (d) banner width within ±4px of live, (e) dot count === slide count, (f) autoplay advances over 4s, (g) the two nav bars have the correct separate items. Save screenshots `team-log/screens/clone_r2_1440.png` + `clone_r2_1920.png`. 5. Report: files changed, build exit code, the corrected two-bar structure (vehicle-type tabs + category sub-nav with exact items), and the self-check numbers. Do NOT write an eval report — the evaluator re-runs after. Keep Spanish labels verbatim. Surgical scope (above-the-fold only). Report when built + builds clean + self-checks pass.
